_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 2010 batch)
_meta.note:
VA SPOTSYLVANIA LOCAL 5-pack — completing the Spotsy School Board + the BoS Chair: jennifer-craig-ford (Battlefield, sworn Jan 2026), lawrence-a-dibella-iii (Berkeley, sworn Jan 2026), lori-hayes (BoS Lee Hill Chairman since Jan 2026), lorita-c-daniels (Salem, D running for US Senate D primary vs Warner June 16 2026), megan-jackson (Livingston, Chair Jan 2025-present). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Craig-Ford — iVoterGuide 'Leans Liberal,' declined ~20 culture-war questions; HARD TRUE public_justice q0 March 2026 push to RESTORE FBI Rap Back continuous-criminal-monitoring of school employees from FY27 budget (paired with DiBella); SOFT FALSE economic_stewardship q2 supports 'strong public funding for education from Pre-K through college' (iVoterGuide); SOFT FALSE public_justice q3 her safety platform is conflict-resolution / SEL framing ('Teaching students... resolve conflicts peacefully'); SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q1 agreed teachers should instruct state-mandated curriculum despite personal beliefs (iVoterGuide); most other cells NULL — she declined ~20 questions which under cite-only discipline leaves them honestly blank. (2) DiBella — Spotsy GOP-endorsed (party Unknown→R), iVoterGuide 'Leans Conservative,' career 18-year Spotsy Sheriff's Office First Sergeant + 13-year School Resource Officer including Riverbend HS 2015 active-shooter-plot foil; HARD TRUE public_justice q0 on multi-vector basis (career SRO + Riverbend save + 'School Safety' top priority); SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2 motion to cut ~20% from top-of-market admin/principal/central-office pay to redirect to teacher salaries (motion rebuffed by majority); SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q3 May 2026 'This is absolutely not OK' opposition (with Lieberman) to Supt Mitchell's altered policy BHA-R; he did NOT respond to iVoterGuide on most culture-war items → many cells NULL by discipline. (3) Hayes — ROSTER FIX: ballot party I (Independent, NOT R as record showed) — both 2021 + 2025 wins were as Independent — but she is the de-facto conservative-coalition supervisor per FFP characterization ('conservative Lee Hill District Supervisor Lori Hayes,' FFP 2024-04-10) so keeping party R per real-world-coalition convention parallel to Yakabouski/Frazier; OFFICE STRING fix to match Spotsy BoS convention ('County Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)'). HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2 FY25 NO vote on 73.43¢ tax rate because TOO HIGH — she + Yakabouski + Frazier the 3 NO votes; Hayes was the LOWEST-TAX position (Yakabouski/Frazier wanted higher), corrects the prior 1955 batch's partition. HARD TRUE self_defense q1 + refuse_state_overreach q4 — as BoS Chair she ADDED 2A discussion to Jan 29 2026 agenda ('imperative... because of how quickly things are coming down') and the board RE-AFFIRMED 2A Sanctuary status, signaling sheriff non-enforcement of state assault-weapon restrictions advancing in General Assembly. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q3 RTA-pushback subsidiarity. SOFT TRUE public_justice q0 (sheriff-funding coalition + 2A leadership). SOFT TRUE family_child_sovereignty q2 — CRRL Board service addressing parents' concern over sexually explicit content auto-pushed to a child's library-app tablet feed (parental-controls advocacy on public-library content). CHAIR TENURE: Jan 2026-present (NOT 2024 as scorecard implied — predecessor Chair was Jacob Lane 2024). (4) Daniels — D-aligned + announced Nov 12 2025 D primary challenge to Sen. Mark Warner for US Senate 2026 (party Unknown→D, candidacy_status running_higher_office). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2 + q4 — Aug 15 2023 voted NO on Youngkin VDOE model policy requiring biological-sex bathrooms/pronouns. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 — voted YES (5-0) Jan 2024 to RESTORE 37 previously-removed books to libraries; voted NO May 2023 on 4-3 'sexually explicit content' definition for book challenges; rejected library opt-out form 2024. HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0 — voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor; Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 2024) alleges retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair (HARD on vote; board's stated cause was unlicensed hiring + FOIA + social-media conduct, but the Christian-expression nexus is in the public federal complaint, so direction-FALSE on christian_liberty q0 follows). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — voted YES on FY25 expansionary budget (+$44M YoY to $479.9M total, $418M operating) in the 4-3 expansionary coalition (Daniels/Cole/Jackson/Medawar/Rodas). CHAIR: Jan 2024-Jan 2025 (lost chair vote 4-3 to Megan Jackson Jan 2025). (5) Jackson — D-coalition Chair (party Unknown→D, candidacy_status incumbent), unseated Twigg 'burn books' faction Nov 2023 (57.97%-34.47%-7.1% over Twigg + Zuponeck-Carlson). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2 — voted YES (5-2 majority with Daniels/Cole/Medawar/Rodas) Jan 2024 to ROLL BACK Twigg-era explicit-book-removal policy, restoring books and giving 'superintendent and principals' discretion rather than parent-driven removal. HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0 — voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor over Christmas Christian book fair federal-lawsuit nexus. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2 — as Chair presided over FY26 5-0 budget adoption $485.8M (expansion). HARD FALSE self_defense q1 — Nov 2025 5-0 vote to RESTORE gun-free-zone designation for admin building (gun-free-zone affirmation is rubric-adverse on q1). SOFT FALSE public_justice q2 — voted DOWN third-party HR audit after troubling hiring incidents (April 2025), opposite of proportional-accountability direction. SOFT FALSE christian_liberty q2 — Nov 7 2025 no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from all SCPS property through July 1 2026 (FIRE opened a free-speech case); content-control direction on free-speech-at-board. CONFIRMS the D-direction post-Twigg-flip pattern cleanly across all 5 high-salience cells.

RICK-WEST 5TH-CONSECUTIVE-DEFER (urgent escalation): rick-west (VA Mayor of Chesapeake, R, level=local, TOP of LOCAL priority queue) remains slug-collided with rick-west (OK State Representative, R, level=state) — engine `by_slug` lookup is last-wins by bare slug (refine-records.py line 366), so a dossier keyed at `rick-west` would mutate the OK State Rep record (last in order), NOT the VA Mayor. This is now the FIFTH consecutive batch to defer him (0950, 1047, 1441, 1625 on 2026-06-02 + 1550 on 2026-06-04 + this 2010 = the 1955 batch did NOT defer Rick West because it was a Spotsy BoS 5-pack that didn't touch him). Research COMPLETE and stashed; would score ~67/100 (HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4 May 2024 first-ever Chesapeake Pride proclamation, FALSE refuse_overreach q2 COVID mask/distance/vax encouragement, TRUE econ_q2 unanimous 9-0 FY24 4¢ RE-tax cut, TRUE pj_q0 affirmative LE-pay-raise + Jefferies nomination, TRUE border q2 2017 8-1 anti-sanctuary vote, TRUE self_defense q1 Dec 2019 unanimous 2A Constitutional City presidency). REQUEST (re-issued, escalating): please rename VA `rick-west` slug to `rick-west-chesapeake` so the engine can target him distinctly — proposed because OK Rick West is at archetype/party-default with no real differentiation, while VA Rick West is a Mayor with rich evidence ready to apply. This is now blocking a top-priority, fully-researched VA Mayor scorecard for the FIFTH straight cron fire — escalating from a 'request' to a 'blocker.'

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — neither campaign site nor board bio nor BallotReady nor Ballotpedia nor any press coverage discloses a denomination or church affiliation.
profile.education: NO PUBLIC DISCLOSURE — neither campaign site nor official board bio states an undergraduate or law degree, though her professional identity as attorney is public (likely CA Bar #239768, 'Jennifer Morgan Craig-Ford'; now practices in VA).
profile.background: Attorney + local business owner. Mother of four; current Courtland HS parent; hosted four international students through SCPS. Has a niece who is a school-shooting survivor (cited as motivating her safety platform). Self-described early-life experience of homelessness 'saved by quality teachers' frames her pro-public-school stance. Civic record: Cub Scouts Den Leader; advocate for IEP families. Won Nov 4 2025 Spotsylvania School Board Battlefield District 3-way race for the seat vacated by Nicole Cole (D, who ran for HD-66 vs Bobby Orrock): Craig-Ford 2,804 plurality (~36%) | James King (D-endorsed pastor) 2,414 | Nick Ignacio (former Fredericksburg Regional Tea Party chair) 2,285. Sworn in January 2026 alongside Larry DiBella (Berkeley) and Rich Lieberman (Lee Hill); 4-year term ends Dec 31 2029. Ran near-zero-money campaign ($25 total raised, single donor as of July 2025 per FXBG Advance campaign finance reporting). Officially nonpartisan ballot; neither Spotsy GOP nor Spotsy Dems endorsed her; explicitly self-described as the 'remove politics from the School Board' middle-lane candidate ('I've been running nonpartisan from the beginning'; 'I've been accused of being a covert Democrat and a covert Republican').

jennifer-craig-ford.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Battlefield District, sworn January 2026 at Stevenson Ridge ceremony, term ends 12/31/2029. Won 3-way race Nov 4 2025 with 36% plurality between a D-endorsed pastor and a former Tea Party chair. PARTY FIX: ballot Independent (nonpartisan); set to I; iVoterGuide rated her 'Leans Liberal' after she declined ~20 substantive questions on parental rights, library content, sex-ed, biological sex in sports/facilities, prayer, school choice, eliminating DOE, election integrity, and constitutional matters. RELIGION: not disclosed publicly — null. EDUCATION: not publicly disclosed beyond attorney identity (likely CA Bar #239768). FAMILY: mother of 4; niece is school-shooting survivor (motivates safety platform). HARD TRUE public_justice q0: March 2026 publicly objected to cutting the FBI Rap Back continuous-criminal-monitoring program ($100K for school employees) from the FY27 budget — paired with Larry DiBella (Sheriff's First Sergeant) in pushing to restore it. Pro-LE-vetting / pro-child-safety affirmative advocacy. SOFT FALSE economic_stewardship q2: iVoterGuide answer (https://ivoterguide.com/candidate/88522/race/23297/election/1312) — supports 'strong public funding for education from Pre-K through college,' citing global competitiveness/workforce; center-left funding posture, not local fiscal restraint (and the rubric direction for q2 is opposing deficit spending). SOFT FALSE public_justice q3: campaign Safe Neighborhoods plank emphasizes 'Teaching our students the skills they need to express themselves effectively and resolve conflicts peacefully' (https://craigford4schoolboard.org/) — SEL / conflict-resolution / restorative framing of school safety, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 ('refuses to treat violent crime as a public-health excuse to avoid punishment'). SOFT FALSE refuse_state_overreach q1: iVoterGuide — agreed teachers should instruct state-mandated curriculum despite personal beliefs (deference to state mandates rather than push back, opposite direction of q1). NOT scored on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (declined iVoterGuide on biological sex in sports/facilities), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (declined iVoterGuide on parental consent, sex-ed opt-in/out, school choice; no library/book vote on record yet since sworn Jan 2026), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no statements on prayer/invocation, Christian-club access, religious-liberty defense), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (declined), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A), self_defense q0/q1 (her safety frame is conflict-resolution, not arming; no firearms-on-grounds vote since sworn). GOVERNANCE FLAG (not a rubric cell): May 12 2026 on Policy BHA-R (requiring superintendent approval for board-member school visits), Craig-Ford moved to TABLE the vote — motion failed 4-3 — then voted FOR final passage (5-2 first reading) siding with the Jackson/Mitchell/admin majority over the DiBella/Lieberman opposition bloc. Net 1 TRUE / 3 FALSE on cited cells = 25%. Coherent center / nonpartisan with center-left tilt on funding and state-curriculum deference; one genuine pro-LE/child-safety crossover win on the FBI Rap Back push. Recommend revisiting after she casts her first FY27 budget vote, any Title IX policy votes, and the $237.2M bond referendum.

2.note: iVoterGuide answer: supports 'strong public funding for education from Pre-K through college,' citing global competitiveness/workforce — center-left funding posture, the rubric-adverse direction on q2's oppose-deficit-spending axis. Has not yet cast a Spotsy SB budget vote since sworn Jan 2026.

0.note: March 2026: publicly objected to cutting the FBI Rap Back continuous-criminal-monitoring program for school employees ($100K) from the FY27 budget — paired with Larry DiBella (Sheriff's First Sergeant) in pushing to restore it. Affirmative pro-LE-vetting / pro-child-safety advocacy aligns with 'publicly backed law enforcement' direction on q0.

3.note: Campaign Safe Neighborhoods plank: 'Teaching our students the skills they need to express themselves effectively and resolve conflicts peacefully' — SEL / conflict-resolution / restorative framing of school safety, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 ('refuses to treat violent crime as a public-health excuse to avoid punishment').

1.note: iVoterGuide answer: agreed teachers should instruct state-mandated curriculum despite personal beliefs — deference to state mandates, the rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('declined state strings that compel ungodly or unconstitutional local policy').

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND of self-declared denomination or church. (A Phyllis DiBella serves as Assistant Principal at Fredericksburg Christian School per public LinkedIn, but the family relationship is NOT publicly confirmed; flagged as a lead, not a claim.)
profile.education: NO PUBLIC DISCLOSURE — neither campaign site, official board bio, FXBG Advance questionnaire, nor iVoterGuide profile state college credentials.
profile.background: First Sergeant, Spotsylvania County Sheriff's Office (18 years); 13-year veteran School Resource Officer; assigned to Riverbend HS in October 2015 where his investigation was credited with thwarting a planned mass-shooting plot (CNN 2015-10-25; Spotsy Sheriff's Office). Lost the Nov 7 2023 Spotsylvania SB Battlefield District race to Nicole Cole by ~3 points (campaign domain dibellaforbattlefield.com). Won Nov 4 2025 Spotsylvania SB Berkeley District 58.56% (4,909) vs Amanda M. Monroe 40.13% (3,364) for the open seat vacated by April Gillespie. Sworn in January 2026 at the Stevenson Ridge ceremony alongside Jennifer Craig-Ford (Battlefield) and Rich Lieberman (Lee Hill); 4-year term ends Dec 31 2029. Endorsed by Spotsylvania County Republican Committee + Rappahannock Conservative Women's Coalition + Fredericksburg Area Young Republicans (April 2026 public thanks). iVoterGuide rated 'Leans Conservative' but he did NOT complete the questionnaire — most culture-war positions remain unsignaled.

lawrence-a-dibella-iii.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Berkeley District, sworn January 2026, term ends 12/31/2029. PARTY FIX: ballot Independent (nonpartisan SB) → real-world R-coalition (Spotsy GOP endorsement + Spotsy GOP Committee contributions + Rappahannock Conservative Women's Coalition + Fredericksburg Area Young Republicans April 2026 public thanks for 'expanding Career and Technical Education opportunities and advocating for drug testing of those with direct contact for our students'). Per scorecard convention (parallel to Yakabouski/Frazier/Goosman tagged R/D rather than I despite Independent ballots): party set to R. RELIGION: not publicly self-declared — null (Phyllis DiBella at Fredericksburg Christian School is a lead but not confirmed family relationship). EDUCATION: not publicly disclosed. CAREER: 18-year Spotsy Sheriff's Office First Sergeant + 13-year SRO; 2015 Riverbend HS active-shooter-plot foil (CNN cite). CRITICAL CONTEXT: Sworn Jan 2026 → did NOT vote on 2021-2024 Spotsy SB flashpoints (Twigg 'burn books' 2021, Mark Taylor hire/firing 2022-24, Aug 2023 Youngkin VDOE model-policy adoption, May 2023 'sexually explicit content' definition, Jan 2024 book restoration, 2024 opt-out form rejection, 2024 transgender policy). All those cells null on his record — not scored from later coalition. HARD TRUE public_justice q0: 13-year career SRO + Riverbend HS active-shooter foil + 'School Safety' top campaign priority + Fredericksburg Free Press 2025-10-24 quote 'we need to do everything we can to make our schools safer, including working more closely with law enforcement partners, ensuring we have the best safety equipment and updating appropriate policies' — multi-vector affirmative LE advocacy, clears the FXBG-discipline high-bar. SOFT TRUE economic_stewardship q2: led motion to cut ~20% from top-of-market admin / principal / central-office pay to redirect to teacher salaries — 'There are several categories where we are well above market, particularly with our assistant principals, our principals, and some of the central office level staff' (motion failed; majority rebuffed). Fiscal-restraint instinct on board governance even if outvoted. SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q3: May 12 2026 on Policy BHA-R (board-member-school-visits requiring Superintendent approval) said 'This is absolutely not OK' and accused Supt Clint Mitchell of altering the policy post-April-20 board review — local-board-control assertion against superintendent overreach (q3 subsidiarity direction on board-versus-administrator authority). NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1, biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (no votes on Pride / pronouns / trans-bathroom since sworn; pre-2025 votes predate tenure), family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (drug-testing-teachers push is workforce-vetting not parental-notification on minors' interventions; CTE expansion not specifically a school-choice or anti-CRT vote — declined iVoterGuide on all parental rights items), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (no statements on prayer/invocation, Christian-club access, religious-liberty defense; church affiliation undisclosed), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (N/A), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (only q0 SRO cleared), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q4 (no VDOE/COVID/ESG-refusal statement), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A school office), self_defense q0/q1 (Christian-conservative anti-gun-free-zone direction not specifically advocated; hardened-schools posture is partnership-with-LE not constitutional-carry). 3 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells = 100% (sparse-data caveat: denominator essentially 3, reflects research diligence on a 5-month-old SB tenure with iVoterGuide-decline pattern). Coherent textbook GOP-endorsed sheriff-deputy/SRO reformer-conservative profile in the conservative minority on a board flipped to D-majority Jan 2026 (Jackson Chair, Rodas Vice Chair, Daniels-Cole-Medawar). Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget vote, the $237.2M bond referendum, any 2027 Title IX or library-content votes.

0.note: HIGH-BAR cleared on multiple vectors: (1) 13-year career School Resource Officer; (2) personally credited with foiling a 2015 active-shooter plot at Riverbend HS (CNN 2015-10-25); (3) campaign top-priority 'School Safety'; (4) explicit pro-LE-partnership quote 'we need to do everything we can to make our schools safer, including working more closely with law enforcement partners, ensuring we have the best safety equipment and updating appropriate policies.' Clear affirmative LE advocacy on multiple independent vectors.

2.note: Led motion to cut ~20% from top-of-market administrator / principal / central-office pay to redirect to teacher salaries: 'There are several categories where we are well above market, particularly with our assistant principals, our principals, and some of the central office level staff.' Motion rebuffed by majority. Fiscal-restraint instinct on internal board governance even when outvoted.

3.note: May 12 2026 on Policy BHA-R (board-member-school-visits requiring Supt approval): 'This is absolutely not OK' and accused Supt Clint Mitchell of altering the policy post-April-20 board review — local-board-control assertion against superintendent overreach (q3 subsidiarity direction on board-versus-administrator authority within the local body).

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — hayes4supervisor.com About page and Free Lance-Star / Fredericksburg Free Press profiles do not state a church affiliation. The prior 'Christian' tag in the record is unverified and should not be claimed without a source.
profile.education: B.A. Social Work, George Mason University; also attended Germanna Community College and Northern Virginia Community College.
profile.background: Born Michigan; moved to Fredericksburg area 1982 and graduated from James Monroe HS 1983; returned to Spotsylvania 2005. Sole owner of Calypso Kennels in Stafford County (opened 1990), co-owned with her brother until his death in 2023. Husband David; one son who graduated Spotsylvania County Public Schools (Massaponax HS marching band). Civic: Stafford Rotary (Youth & Community Service Chair); former 1st VP, Massaponax Band Parent Association; one of two Spotsylvania representatives on the Central Rappahannock Regional Library (CRRL) Board of Trustees. First elected Lee Hill District Supervisor Nov 2 2021 defeating Todd Alan Rump 61%-39% as an Independent (per VA Dept of Elections historical record). Re-elected Nov 4 2025 against challenger Bruce Garcia (4,306 to 2,972) — also Independent ballot. Elevated to Board Chair at the January 2026 BoS reorganization (predecessor Chair was Jacob Lane / Livingston in 2024). Current term Jan 2026 – Dec 2029; next election Nov 2029.

lori-hayes.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania BoS Lee Hill District, first elected Nov 2021, re-elected Nov 2025, elevated to Board Chair Jan 2026 reorganization (CORRECTION: scorecard had implied chair since 2024 but predecessor Chair was Jacob Lane in 2024). Term ends Dec 31 2029. OFFICE STRING FIX: 'Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)' → 'County Board of Supervisors — Lee Hill District (Chairman)' to match the Spotsy BoS convention from prior 1955 batch (Yakabouski/Goosman/Childress/Mullins/Lane/Frazier all use 'County Board of Supervisors — <District> District' prefix). PARTY: ballot is Independent (both 2021 and 2025 wins) but Free Press explicitly labels her 'conservative Lee Hill District Supervisor Lori Hayes' (FFP 2024-04-10) and she coalitions with Childress/Lane/Mullins on fiscal-restraint votes; keeping R per real-world-coalition convention parallel to other Spotsy BoS members (all of whom ran Independent on ballot but are scored R/D in the record). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed — null (clearing the prior unverified 'Christian' tag per cite-only discipline). EDUCATION: BA Social Work George Mason. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: April 9 2024 FY25 BoS vote on 73.43¢ real-estate tax rate was 4-3; Hayes voted NO because the rate was TOO HIGH (FFP 2024-04-10: 'the rate that was approved Tuesday night was too high for conservative Lee Hill District Supervisor Lori Hayes... Hayes, Battlefield District Supervisor Chris Yakabouski and Deborah Frazier of the Salem District voted against the motion'). CORRECTS the prior 1955 batch's 4-3 partition: the actual 4 YES votes were Childress/Lane/Mullins/Goosman (the conservative-restraint majority), and the 3 NO votes were Hayes (too high), Yakabouski (too low), Frazier (too low) — Hayes is the LOWEST-TAX position on the entire board, the 'right of the right' NO. This is multi-cycle fiscal restraint: she also pushed senior-tax-relief expansion for FY26 ('Our job up here is to take our whole citizenry into account, not just one segment,' FFP 2025-06-18 / 2024-05-16). HARD TRUE self_defense q1 + HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4: as newly-elected Board Chair, Hayes ADDED 2A discussion to the Jan 29 2026 BoS agenda stating it was 'imperative... because of how quickly things are coming down,' and the board RE-AFFIRMED Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary County status, signaling sheriff non-enforcement of state assault-weapon restrictions advancing in the Virginia General Assembly (FFP 2026-01-30). Initiating (not just along-voting) the re-affirmation = HARD on q1 (opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, 'assault weapons' bans) AND on refuse_state_overreach q4 (refused to enforce unconstitutional state directives). SOFT TRUE refuse_state_overreach q3: led BoS opposition to Del. Joshua Cole's bill forcing Spotsylvania into a Regional Transportation Authority — 'Spotsylvania board, in its legislative priorities, requested that the state not force it into an RTA' (FFP 2026-01-30) — subsidiarity direction. SOFT TRUE public_justice q0: sheriff-funding coalition + 2A re-affirmation leadership signal pro-LE advocacy (HIGH-BAR cleared by 2A-sanctuary initiation more than by routine sheriff line-item funding). SOFT TRUE family_child_sovereignty q2: as CRRL Board of Trustees member, Hayes addressed parents' concern over sexually explicit content auto-pushed to a child's library-app tablet feed; said the board's responsibility is to address public concerns; 'This was never about censorship' — supported parental-controls fix to the app (parental-controls advocacy on public-library content, q2 anti-SOGI/anti-comprehensive-sex-ed adjacent on age-appropriateness axis). FY27 caveat (NOT scored on a single cell, kept in notes for context): Feb 25 2026 BoS as Chair allowed an advertised rate of 77¢ — equal to last year nominally but ~8¢ above the equalized rate due to reassessments. NOT a final budget vote (work session / public hearing set March 2026), so the FY27 final-vote fault line is not yet in the record. Will be the cleanest re-score signal next batch. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no pro-life-resolution / abortion-sanctuary vote), biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 (Spotsy BoS did not pass a Pride proclamation in years searched), family_child_sovereignty q0 (school choice ESA position not on record), family_child_sovereignty q1 (no medical/gender-intervention-consent vote), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (invocation/Nativity/religious-liberty-defense / faith-identity all undocumented), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (no voter ID / paper ballots / Zuckerbucks-refusal vote on her record), public_justice q1/q2/q3/q4 (bail reform / RICO / prosecutor oversight / soft-on-crime pushback undocumented), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2 (no specific COVID-era refusal vote since 2021 — she took office mid-pandemic but pandemic-era votes mostly predate her tenure), border_immigration q1/q2 (no 287(g) / sanctuary vote located on the BoS record; Spotsy Sheriff sets immigration policy separately), self_defense q0 (no specific constitutional-carry advocacy beyond the 2A sanctuary). Net 4 TRUE / 0 FALSE on cited cells = 100% on cited (with the q2-FY27-advertised-rate caveat preserved in notes). Coherent low-key conservative-servant-leader profile: low-key on culture-war signaling, HARD on fiscal restraint (lowest-tax NO on FY25 4-3), HARD on 2A (Chair-initiated sanctuary re-affirmation), confirmed R-coalition. Recommend revisiting after the FY27 final-vote partition lands (March 2026), the FY28 budget cycle, and any 2A / immigration / library-content actions in 2026-27.

2.note: April 9 2024 FY25 BoS 4-3 vote on the 73.43¢ real-estate tax rate: Hayes voted NO because the rate was TOO HIGH (FFP 2024-04-10 explicitly: 'the rate that was approved Tuesday night was too high for conservative Lee Hill District Supervisor Lori Hayes... Hayes, Battlefield District Supervisor Chris Yakabouski and Deborah Frazier of the Salem District voted against the motion'). LOWEST-TAX position on the entire board — the right-of-the-right NO. Multi-cycle restraint: also pushed senior-tax-relief expansion for FY26 ('Our job up here is to take our whole citizenry into account, not just one segment').

1.note: As newly-elected Board Chair, Hayes ADDED 2A discussion to the Jan 29 2026 BoS agenda stating it was 'imperative... because of how quickly things are coming down,' and the board RE-AFFIRMED Spotsylvania's 2A Sanctuary County status — signaling sheriff non-enforcement of Virginia General Assembly assault-weapon restrictions then advancing. INITIATION (not along-vote) of the re-affirmation as Chair = HARD on q1 (opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, 'assault weapons' bans, gun registries).

3.note: Led BoS opposition to Del. Joshua Cole's bill forcing Spotsylvania into a Regional Transportation Authority — 'Spotsylvania board, in its legislative priorities, requested that the state not force it into an RTA' (Jan 29 2026). Subsidiarity direction on q3 — decisions at the lowest competent level.

4.note: Jan 29 2026 Chair-initiated 2A Sanctuary re-affirmation explicitly signals refusal to enforce VA General Assembly state-level assault-weapon restrictions — q4 'refused to enforce unconstitutional state directives' direction.

0.note: Chair-initiated 2A Sanctuary re-affirmation + sheriff-funding coalition signals pro-LE advocacy (HIGH BAR cleared by INITIATION of the sanctuary re-affirmation rather than routine sheriff line-item funding).

2.note: As CRRL Board of Trustees member (one of two Spotsy reps), Hayes addressed parents' concern over sexually explicit content auto-pushed to a child's library-app tablet feed; framed the board's responsibility as addressing public concerns; 'This was never about censorship' — supported a parental-controls fix to the app. Parental-controls advocacy on public-library content aligns with the q2 anti-SOGI / anti-comprehensive-sex-ed direction on age-appropriateness.

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — drdanielsforschoolboard.com and loritadaniels.com (Senate campaign) bios are silent on denomination or church affiliation.
profile.education: Ph.D. in Public Administration and Policy, Virginia Tech; M.P.A., Valdosta State University.
profile.background: Federal employee + adjunct university professor + former substitute teacher (rural Georgia) + former PTA president + small-business founder. Military spouse (Air Force officer husband); son graduated Spotsylvania County Public Schools (Purple Star family). Immediate Past Commissioner, Virginia Commission for the Arts; Healthy Families Board. First elected Spotsylvania School Board, Salem District, Nov 2018; re-elected Nov 7 2023 (55.57% vs Harris 43.83%) for a 4-year term ending Dec 31 2027. Served as Board Chair Jan 2024 – Jan 2025 (lost the chair vote 4-3 to Megan Jackson in Jan 2025). Announced Nov 12 2025 (Veterans Day) campaign for U.S. Senate as a Democrat, challenging Sen. Mark Warner in the June 16 2026 D primary. Will remain in the SB Salem seat through end of current term in 2027 regardless of Senate primary outcome.

lorita-c-daniels.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Salem District since Jan 2019 (elected 2018), re-elected 2023, term ends 12/31/2027. Chair Jan 2024 – Jan 2025. PARTY FIX: Unknown → D (HARD: filed for 2026 Democratic U.S. Senate primary challenging Sen. Warner). CANDIDACY STATUS: running_higher_office (sitting SB + 2026 D primary U.S. Senate candidate). OFFICE STRING augmented to flag dual-track status. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed in either SB or Senate campaign material — null. EDUCATION: Ph.D. Public Admin & Policy from Virginia Tech (the 'Dr.' prefix is legitimate, earned, not honorific) + MPA Valdosta State. DEVIATION FLAG: school-system-insider (Ph.D. in policy + adjunct prof + PTA + substitute teacher) on a board that flashpoint-flipped over parental-rights battles. COALITION: D-aligned reformer bloc (Daniels-Cole-Jackson-Medawar-Rodas) that ousted the 2021-23 Twigg-Taylor-Abuismail conservative majority. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: Aug 15 2023 Spotsy SB voted 4-2 (Shelley abstaining) to adopt the Youngkin VDOE model transgender policy requiring biological-sex bathrooms / pronouns / parental notification — Daniels voted NO, citing Title IX liability ('to make sure that we are clear what we're doing, because if we implement something that goes against Title IX, [that] is a liability'). Voting NO on biological-sex affirmation is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 (TRUE = rejects transgender ideology and affirms biological sex). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: same Aug 15 2023 NO vote on the Youngkin model policy is a vote AGAINST policy opposing LGBTQ+ promotion in schools, which is the q4 'opposes promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy, schools' direction. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted YES (5-0) Jan 2024 to RESTORE 37 previously-removed books to school libraries (the Twigg-era removal had targeted sexually-explicit content); voted NO May 2023 on the 4-3 'sexually explicit content' definition added to book-challenge regulations (called it 'incomplete and misleading'); under her chairship the board rejected an opt-out form for library books in 2024. Rolling back parental-controls / content-restrictions on K-12 library content is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes CRT, SOGI, comprehensive sex ed, and gender-ideology curricula in K-12 public schools'). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q1: rejection of the library opt-out form weakens parental notification + consent on age-sensitive content (q1 'supports parental notification and consent on all... interventions for minors' direction). HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0: voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor for cause; Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 8 2024 per Fox 5 DC) alleges the firing was retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair promoting Christian literature. Daniels' own quote: 'We just needed to move this district forward'; she initially abstained, then changed her vote to YES in closed session ('I felt better about how we were moving forward with cause'). The board's stated cause was unlicensed hiring + FOIA violations + social-media conduct (not the book fair), but the Christian-expression nexus is in the public federal complaint, supporting a FALSE direction on christian_liberty q0 ('right to publicly profess Christ in all spheres... schools'). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on the FY25 Spotsy SB expansionary budget — total $479.9M, operating $418M (+$44M YoY) — in the 4-3 expansionary coalition vote (Daniels/Cole/Jackson/Medawar/Rodas YES); opposite direction from the rubric's oppose-deficit-spending / fiscal-restraint axis on q2. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1 (no abortion or sex-ed-curriculum statement; Senate campaign silent thus far), biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement located), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice / homeschool-freedom statement on record — board has not voted on school-choice partnerships under her tenure), christian_liberty q2/q3/q4 (no compelled-speech / public-square-Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display votes located), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (SB office; declined), public_justice q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (DEI quote at May 2025 Spotsy NAACP forum maps to family_child_sovereignty q2 direction not public_justice; no SRO / bail / prosecutor-oversight statement located; bullying-policy specifics not located), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (her Aug 2023 NO vote was DEFERENCE to federal Title IX, NOT local-control assertion against state mandates in the conservative direction — null on refuse_state_overreach rather than scoring backward), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A school office), self_defense q0/q1 (no armed-SRO / hardened-school / firearm-policy vote located on her record). Net 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% on cited — coherent D-aligned reformer-coalition pattern across all five high-salience local axes: voted DOWN biological-sex policy, voted UP book restoration, voted UP Supt-firing tied to Christian-expression lawsuit, voted UP expansionary budget. Recommend revisiting after the June 16 2026 D primary outcome (winner or loser, candidacy_status will shift), the FY27 SB budget vote, and any 2026 Senate-campaign positions on abortion / marriage / education that may surface.

2.note: Aug 15 2023: Spotsy SB voted 4-2 (Shelley abstaining) to adopt the Youngkin VDOE model transgender policy requiring biological-sex bathrooms / pronouns / parental notification — Daniels voted NO, citing Title IX liability. Voting AGAINST biological-sex affirmation is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('rejects transgender ideology and affirms biological sex... as immutable and God-given').

Daniels' own quotes: 'We just needed to move this district forward'; she initially abstained then changed to YES in closed session ('I felt better about how we were moving forward with cause'). profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — campaign materials, board bio, FXBG Advance election-guide profile, and Potomac Local coverage do not disclose a denomination or church affiliation.
profile.education: NO PUBLIC DISCLOSURE — neither campaign site nor official board bio nor election coverage discloses college credentials.
profile.background: First-time candidate Nov 2023; mother of children in Spotsylvania County Public Schools. School-safety platform pillar credited to the 2018 Parkland shooting impact. Defeated incumbent Kirk Twigg (the much-publicized 'burn books' board member) Nov 7 2023 with 57.97% (vs Twigg 34.47%, Zuponeck-Carlson 7.1%) for the Livingston District seat. Sworn in January 2024; 4-year term ends Dec 31 2027 (not on the Nov 2025 or Nov 2026 ballot). Elected Board Chair Jan 2025 (4-3 vote, replacing Lorita Daniels); re-elected Chair Jan 2026. Profession not definitively confirmed in public sources reviewed.

megan-jackson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania SB Livingston District since Jan 2024 (elected Nov 7 2023), term ends 12/31/2027 — NOT on Nov 2025 or Nov 2026 ballot. Chair since Jan 2025 (replaced Daniels 4-3), re-elected Chair Jan 2026. PARTY FIX: Unknown → D (real-world D-coalition; ballot nonpartisan but coalitions with Daniels/Cole/Medawar/Rodas reformer bloc that ousted the 2021-2023 Twigg/Taylor/Abuismail conservative majority; FXBG Advance 2025 election guide explicitly classifies the seat she holds as a 'pro-public-school candidate' seat). RELIGION + EDUCATION: not publicly disclosed — null. DEVIATION FLAGS: signed Nov 7 2025 no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from all SCPS property through July 1 2026; FIRE (Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression) opened a free-speech case against the board (fire.org); conservative outlet Spotsy Wire labels her 'Marxist Megan Jackson.' Voted YES on March 12 2024 firing of Christian-conservative Supt Mark Taylor (his federal lawsuit alleges retaliation for a Christian book fair). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted YES in 5-2 majority (Jackson, Daniels, Cole, Medawar, Rodas YES; Gillespie, Phelps NO) ROLLING BACK Twigg-era explicit-book-removal policy — new framework gives 'superintendent and principals' discretion rather than parent-driven removal. Rolling back parental-controls on K-12 library content is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes CRT, SOGI, comprehensive sex ed, and gender-ideology curricula in K-12 public schools'). HARD FALSE christian_liberty q0: voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor; Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 8 2024) alleges retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair promoting Christian literature. As Chair-elect at the time of the vote and the public face of the post-Twigg reformer majority, Jackson is on-record for the firing of a superintendent whose federal complaint alleges anti-Christian retaliation — direction FALSE on q0 ('right to publicly profess Christ in all spheres... schools'). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: presided as Chair over FY26 budget 5-0 adoption ($485.8M total, $170.8M local transfer, April 29 2025) — expansionary direction, opposite of the q2 oppose-deficit-spending axis. HARD FALSE self_defense q1: voted YES (5-0) Nov 2025 to RESTORE gun-free-zone designation for the SCPS administration building (paired with rollout of Evolv weapons-detection systems at all 5 high schools + admin building per WJLA / FFP 2025-11-11). Affirming a gun-free-zone designation is the rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('opposes red-flag laws, magazine limits, assault weapons bans, and gun registries' — adjacent direction on disarmament zones). SOFT FALSE public_justice q2: April 17 2025 — voted DOWN third-party HR audit after troubling hiring incidents in the division (FFP 2025-04-17). Voting down accountability infrastructure is opposite of the q2 direction ('supports victim restitution and proportional punishment of evildoers' — adjacent on institutional-accountability axis). SOFT FALSE christian_liberty q2: Nov 7 2025 signed no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from SCPS property through July 1 2026 after ejecting him from Oct 13 2025 meeting for 'racist rhetoric'; FIRE opened a free-speech case (fire.org). Jackson's own quote: 'The right to free speech is protected under the law, however, this right must be exercised in a manner that preserves the decorum...' Content-control on a hostile critic at public-comment is adjacent to the q2 compelled-speech direction (and FIRE's case framing is that the ban regulates protected expression at a public body). NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0/q1, biblical_marriage q0 (no marriage-definition statement), biblical_marriage q2 (Aug 2023 trans-policy vote PREDATES her Jan 2024 swearing-in — she cannot be scored on that vote), biblical_marriage q4 (no post-Jan-2024 Pride / pronoun / Title IX trans-bathroom vote located on her record), family_child_sovereignty q0 (no school-choice / homeschool statement located), family_child_sovereignty q1 (the opt-out provision adopted under her board provides PARTIAL parental notification — but the rubric direction is opt-in / strong consent for medical/gender interventions, and library content is not medical/gender-intervention; left null per discipline rather than scoring soft-FALSE on a non-clean fit), christian_liberty q3/q4 (no public-square-Christian-symbols / non-Christian-display votes located), election_integrity q0/q1/q4 (SB office; N/A), public_justice q0/q1/q3/q4 (SRO program, bail reform, RICO, prosecutor oversight not on SB jurisdiction), refuse_state_overreach q0/q1/q2/q3/q4 (no COVID votes — pre-tenure; no ESG/equity-audit refusal — voted DOWN HR audit which is OPPOSITE direction; no state-curriculum-refusal votes), border_immigration q1/q2 (N/A school office), self_defense q0 (no constitutional-carry advocacy located). Net 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% on cited — clean D-coalition / institutionalist post-Twigg-flip pattern across all five high-salience local rubric axes: voted UP book restoration, voted UP Supt-firing tied to Christian book fair lawsuit, voted UP expansionary budget, voted UP gun-free zone, voted DOWN HR audit, signed no-trespass order against critic. Recommend revisiting after the FY27 SB budget vote, the $237.2M bond referendum, any 2026-27 Title IX action, and the FIRE free-speech case resolution.

2.note: Voted YES in 5-2 majority (Jackson, Daniels, Cole, Medawar, Rodas YES; Gillespie, Phelps NO) Jan 2024 to ROLL BACK Twigg-era explicit-book-removal policy — new framework gives 'superintendent and principals' discretion rather than parent-driven removal. Rolling back parental-controls on K-12 library content is the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes CRT, SOGI, comprehensive sex ed, and gender-ideology curricula in K-12 public schools').

0.note: Voted YES (5-0) March 12 2024 to fire Supt Mark Taylor. Taylor's federal lawsuit (filed Oct 8 2024) alleges the firing was retaliation for organizing a Dec 2 2023 Christmas Christian-themed book fair promoting Christian literature. As public face of the post-Twigg reformer majority, Jackson is on-record for firing a superintendent whose federal complaint alleges anti-Christian-expression retaliation — direction FALSE on q0 ('right to publicly profess Christ in all spheres... schools').

2.note: Nov 7 2025 signed no-trespass order banning critic Shamgar Connors from SCPS property through July 1 2026 after ejecting him from Oct 13 2025 meeting; FIRE opened a free-speech case against the board. Jackson's quote: 'The right to free speech is protected under the law, however, this right must be exercised in a manner that preserves the decorum...' Content-control on a hostile critic at public-comment is adjacent to the q2 compelled-speech direction in the FIRE-litigation framing.
As Chair, presided over FY26 budget 5-0 adoption ($485.8M total, $170.8M local transfer, April 29 2025) — expansionary direction, the rubric-adverse direction on q2's oppose-deficit-spending axis.

1.note: Voted YES (5-0) Nov 2025 to RESTORE gun-free-zone designation for the SCPS administration building (paired with Evolv weapons-detection rollout at all 5 high schools + admin building). Affirming a gun-free-zone is the rubric-adverse direction on q1 (adjacent to opposing gun-rights infringements via disarmament-zone framing).

2.note: April 17 2025 — voted DOWN third-party HR audit after troubling hiring incidents in the school division. Voting down accountability infrastructure is the rubric-adverse direction on q2's institutional-proportional-accountability axis.
